metadata.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention

one_liner.achieved: vLLM achieves near-zero waste in KV cache memory.
one_liner.via: The authors propose PagedAttention, an attention algorithm inspired by virtual memory and paging techniques.
one_liner.because: General model serving systems fail to take into account the autoregressive property and token state of LLM inference, resulting in missed opportunities for optimization.

claim_tree.statement: General model serving systems fail to take into account the autoregressive property and token state of LLM inference, resulting in missed opportunities for optimization.

children.statement: The authors propose PagedAttention, an attention algorithm inspired by virtual memory and paging techniques.
vLLM achieves near-zero waste in KV cache memory.
vLLM improves the throughput of popular LLMs by 2-4× compared to FasterTransformer and Orca at the same level of latency.

claim_tree.statement: Existing systems struggle with KV cache memory because it is huge and grows/shrinks dynamically.

children.statement: The authors build vLLM, an LLM serving system based on PagedAttention.

claim.statement: The authors build vLLM, an LLM serving system based on PagedAttention.

universal_facets.core_tradeoff: Minimizes KV cache fragmentation to enable higher throughput and batching.
universal_facets.analogy_source: virtual memory and paging techniques in operating systems

systems_facets.s4_mapping: Logical KV cache pages mapped to physical memory via paging
systems_facets.s5_policy: Flexible sharing of KV cache within and across requests

children.statement: vLLM enables flexible sharing of KV cache within and across requests.

claim_tree.statement: Memory waste from fragmentation and redundant duplication limits the batch size.
